steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The second sentence states that the hardingfele produces sound through string vibration. The third sentence states that under the Hornbostel-Sachs system the hardingfele is classified as a chordophone — a class defined by string vibration. The third sentence is a logical conclusion that follows directly from the second.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Instead,: Signals contrast or substitution between two ideas. The chordophone classification does not contradict the sound-production description — it follows from it. ✗
  • Choice B — Furthermore,: Signals addition of a new, separate point. The third sentence is not adding new information; it is drawing a conclusion from the previous sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — Thus,: Signals that what follows is a logical result or conclusion. Because the hardingfele produces sound via string vibration, it therefore (thus) is classified as a chordophone. ✓
  • Choice D — Similarly,: Signals a parallel comparison between two separate items. No second instrument is being compared here — the sentence simply concludes the classification of the hardingfele. ✗
That is the correct answer.
